slides.heading: The 3 Scripts
slides.content: Japanese uses Hiragana (phonetic), Katakana (foreign words), and Kanji (symbols).

slides.heading: Reading Order
slides.content: Most modern Japanese is read left-to-right, just like English books.

slides.heading: The 'A' Row
slides.content: The first five characters are あ (a), い (i), う (u), え (e), お (o).

slides.heading: Visual Mnemonics
slides.content: Try to see shapes in the characters. 'i' (い) looks like two needles.

slides.heading: Practice Daily
slides.content: Even reading just 5 characters a day will help you master the script in one month!
